name: Multiple Sclerosis
summary: Multiple sclerosis is an immune-mediated CNS disease with strong HLA associations and environmental context in population studies. GENARCH provides educational summaries only.
adolescent_relevance: Often begins in young adulthood; earlier-life exposures are discussed in population research.

genetic_architecture.prs_notes: PRS discussed as population-level genetic liability; GENARCH does not provide individual predictions.

exposure_modifiers.mechanism_hypothesis: Vitamin D status proxies are associated with MS risk in some studies; confounding varies.
Smoking is associated with MS outcomes in some cohorts.
Infection exposures are studied as modifiers; pathogen-specific evidence is complex.

population_equity.gwas_ancestry_breakdown: Large MS GWAS have been European-ancestry dominated historically.
population_equity.transferability_notes: HLA mapping and PRS performance can vary across populations.
population_equity.data_gaps: Vitamin D and infection associations are confounded by geography and measurement.

references.title: Genome-wide association meta-analysis of multiple sclerosis identifies risk loci and immune pathways
Vitamin D and multiple sclerosis risk: evidence overview (review)
The Health Consequences of Smoking—50 Years of Progress
references.journal: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services

references.title: Infection exposure and immune-mediated disease mechanisms: overview (review)
